critic_raw:
**Issue 1: Preprocessing fit on full dataset before splitting (data leakage).** Section 3 explicitly states: "all preprocessing and feature engineering transformers are fit on the entire dataset before any data is split." This applies to z-score scaling parameters (global mean and std) and rolling statistics. For a time-series dataset spanning 60 months, fitting normalization on the full dataset leaks future sensor baseline behavior (which may reflect gradual degradation trends) into training normalization. This is particularly impactful in manufacturing sensor data because long-term drift is a primary feature of degradation.

**Issue 2: Stratified random split on 60-month time-series sensor data.** Section 2 describes "stratified random sampling across all available months, holding out a random 15% of observations as the test set." This contradicts the 7-day prediction window: if sensor windows from month 55 appear in training alongside windows from month 5, the model can learn temporal co-occurrence patterns between early and late states of the same equipment, effectively learning failure mode signatures that are only distinguishable when the full lifecycle is visible. A temporal split (train on early months, test on later months) is the correct deployment-mimicking design.

**Issue 3: SPC baseline given no hyperparameter tuning and no equivalent information access.** Section 4 specifies the SPC baseline with a fixed control limit multiplier of 2.0 and a consecutive-violation threshold of 3, stating this "provides a standard reference benchmark without the risk of over-optimizing to a specific time period." The RNN is tuned extensively via grid search; the SPC baseline uses fixed defaults. This asymmetry means the comparison conflates model expressiveness with tuning effort. A fairly-tuned SPC baseline (with multiplier and threshold selected on validation) would provide a more meaningful lower bound. The design acknowledges both models receive equivalent raw data but does not enforce equivalent optimization effort.
all_issues_raised: All preprocessing transformers including z-score scaling are fit on the full 60-month dataset before splitting, leaking long-term sensor drift and degradation trends from future data into training normalization.

Stratified random sampling across 60 months of sensor data assigns future-month windows to training and past-month windows to test, inverting temporal causal order and allowing the model to learn full lifecycle patterns not available at deployment time.

The SPC baseline uses fixed default parameters (control limit multiplier 2.0, threshold 3) while the RNN is tuned via grid search, making the comparison conflate model expressiveness with tuning effort; a validation-tuned SPC would provide a fairer lower bound.
all_issues_adjudicated: All preprocessing transformers including z-score scaling are fit on the full 60-month dataset before splitting, leaking long-term sensor drift and degradation trends from future data into training normalization.

Stratified random sampling across 60 months of sensor data assigns future-month windows to training and past-month windows to test, inverting temporal causal order and allowing the model to learn full lifecycle patterns not available at deployment time.

The SPC baseline uses fixed default parameters (control limit multiplier 2.0, threshold 3) while the RNN is tuned via grid search, making the comparison conflate model expressiveness with tuning effort; a validation-tuned SPC would provide a fairer lower bound.
